name: Japanese Novel
comment: Instant (pre-activation) colorization for .jpnov. Scope families are chosen so VS Code's DEFAULT semantic-typeвЖТscope fallback lands on the SAME colors the LSP semantic layer later emits (commentвЖТcomment, keywordвЖТkeyword вАФ see src/server/semanticTokens.ts HIGHLIGHTS): when the server's tokens arrive they refine invisibly instead of repainting. Every pattern is a single-line `match` (never begin/end), and the tokenizer bounds delimiter pairing to the line too, so both layers agree: an unclosed пЉїпЉГ is DELIBERATELY greyed to its line end (the semantic layer marks the same span as `marker` and the server flags it as an Error), while a lone гАК or a stray пЉљ/гАЛ stays default text. The еВНзВє variant alternation must stay in sync with src/shared/compiler/emphasis.ts DOTS.

patterns.comment: Page break пЉїпЉГжФєгГЪгГЉгВЄпЉљ вАФ frame greyed, directive keyword-colored (semantic: marker/directive).
Emphasis span END пЉїпЉГ(гБЃеЈ¶гБЂ|еЈ¶гБЂ)?<dot>зµВгВПгВКпЉљ вАФ must be tried BEFORE span start (зµВгВПгВК suffix).

Postfix emphasis пЉїпЉГгАМеѓЊи±°гАН(гБЂ|гБЃ)?(гБЃеЈ¶гБЂ|еЈ¶гБЂ)?<dot>пЉљ вАФ the target (group 3) is deliberately UNSCOPED so it keeps the default foreground, exactly like the semantic layer. The target must be NON-empty ([^гАН]+): the tokenizer rejects an empty target (comment fallback), so an empty one must fall through to the generic annotation rule below.

Any other пЉїпЉГвА¶пЉљ (block layout, и¶ЛеЗЇгБЧ, the еВНзЈЪ line family, unknown) вАФ whole annotation greyed, matching the server's `comment` classification. Single-line, so an unclosed пЉїпЉГ never runs away.
patterns.name: comment.block.annotation.novel-jp

patterns.comment: Unterminated пЉїпЉГвА¶ with no пЉљ before the line end вАФ greyed exactly like a closed annotation (the tokenizer swallows the same span as a `brokenAnnotation` and the semantic layer marks it `marker`; the server adds the Error squiggle). $ bounds it to the line; LAST so every closed rule above wins first ([^пЉљ]*$ never matches once a closing пЉљ follows the opener on the line).
patterns.name: comment.block.annotation.novel-jp

patterns.comment: Ruby reading гАКвА¶гАЛ incl. both brackets вАФ greyed like the semantic marker; the base (и¶™жЦЗе≠Ч) before it keeps the default foreground. Empty гАКгАЛ / a lone гАК are left default (the tokenizer treats them literally).
patterns.name: comment.other.ruby.novel-jp

patterns.comment: Explicit ruby base marker пљЬ вАФ the и¶™жЦЗе≠Ч after it stays default.
patterns.name: comment.other.ruby.novel-jp

patterns.comment: Dialogue corner-bracket delimiters вАФ greyed; the utterance content keeps the default foreground (the semantic layer masks it too). Matching per CHARACTER keeps multi-line гАМвА¶гАН stateless and safe.
patterns.name: comment.other.dialogue.novel-jp
